title: Stdlib: complex number functions вЂ” complex, real, imag, conjugate, magnitude, phase
analysis: Task 288 (Complex Value variant) is already merged to main. The core complex functions already exist in reify-stdlib under slightly different names: complex(), re(), im(), conjugate(), phase(), magnitude() (extended for Complex), complex_add(), complex_mul() вЂ” all with comprehensive tests (~60 cases). Task 338 specifies user-facing names that differ from the existing short names: `real()` instead of `re()`, `imag()` instead of `im()`, and `complex_magnitude()` as a dedicated Complex-only magnitude function. The approach is: (1) Add `real`/`imag` as aliases using Rust's `|` pattern in match arms alongside existing `re`/`im` вЂ” e.g. `"re" | "real" =>`, (2) Add `complex_magnitude` as a separate Complex-only function that rejects non-Complex inputs (unlike generic `magnitude` which also handles Tensors), (3) Add task-spec test cases for Voltage dimensions and V/A mismatch, (4) Update LSP completion list (BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS in completion.rs) with all complex function names. All functions follow the existing eval_builtin match-dispatch pattern with unary() helper and sanitize_value() for NaN/Inf guards. The eval_builtin function in lib.rs is the single dispatch point called from reify-expr/src/lib.rs line 91.

prerequisites.description: Verify task 288 Complex variant is present and existing complex tests pass with `cargo test -p reify-stdlib`

steps.description: Write failing tests for `real()` alias in crates/reify-stdlib/src/lib.rs test module: (1) real_dimensionless_returns_real вЂ” eval_builtin("real", &[Complex{3,4,DIMLESS}]) в†’ Real(3.0), (2) real_dimensioned_returns_scalar вЂ” eval_builtin("real", &[Complex{5,3,LENGTH}]) в†’ Scalar{5.0, LENGTH}, (3) real_non_complex_returns_undef вЂ” eval_builtin("real", &[Real(3.0)]) в†’ Undef. These mirror existing re() tests but use the `real` name.

Add `"real"` to the `"re"` match arm using Rust's or-pattern: change `"re" => unary(args, |v| match v {` to `"re" | "real" => unary(args, |v| match v {`. This shares the exact same closure for both names with zero code duplication.

Write failing tests for `imag()` alias: (1) imag_dimensionless_returns_real вЂ” eval_builtin("imag", &[Complex{3,4,DIMLESS}]) в†’ Real(4.0), (2) imag_dimensioned_returns_scalar вЂ” eval_builtin("imag", &[Complex{5,3,LENGTH}]) в†’ Scalar{3.0, LENGTH}, (3) imag_non_complex_returns_undef вЂ” eval_builtin("imag", &[Real(3.0)]) в†’ Undef.
Add `"imag"` to the `"im"` match arm using or-pattern: change `"im" => unary(args, |v| match v {` to `"im" | "imag" => unary(args, |v| match v {`.

Write failing tests for `complex_magnitude()`: (1) complex_magnitude_3_4_returns_5 вЂ” eval_builtin("complex_magnitude", &[Complex{3,4,DIMLESS}]) в†’ Real(5.0), (2) complex_magnitude_dimensioned_returns_scalar вЂ” eval_builtin("complex_magnitude", &[Complex{3,4,LENGTH}]) в†’ Scalar{5.0, LENGTH}, (3) complex_magnitude_non_complex_returns_undef вЂ” eval_builtin("complex_magnitude", &[Real(5.0)]) в†’ Undef (unlike generic magnitude which handles Tensors).

Add `"complex_magnitude"` match arm to eval_builtin: `"complex_magnitude" => unary(args, |v| match v { Value::Complex { re, im, dimension } => { let mag = (re*re + im*im).sqrt(); if *dimension == DimensionVector::DIMENSIONLESS { sanitize_value(Value::Real(mag)) } else { sanitize_value(Value::Scalar { si_value: mag, dimension: *dimension }) } } _ => Value::Undef })`. This is the Complex branch extracted from the generic `magnitude` function, without the Tensor fallback.

Write Voltage dimension spec tests: (1) complex_voltage_preserves_dimension вЂ” build voltage_dim = MASSВ·LENGTHВІВ·TIMEвЃ»ВіВ·CURRENTвЃ»В№, create complex(Scalar{3,voltage}, Scalar{4,voltage}), assert result is Complex with voltage dimension, (2) real_voltage_returns_scalar вЂ” real(complex_voltage) в†’ Scalar{3, voltage}, (3) imag_voltage_returns_scalar вЂ” imag(complex_voltage) в†’ Scalar{4, voltage}, (4) complex_magnitude_voltage вЂ” complex_magnitude(complex_voltage) в†’ Scalar{5, voltage}, (5) conjugate_voltage_preserves_dim вЂ” conjugate flips im sign, preserves voltage dimension. These exercise existing logic with the specific Voltage dimension from the task spec.

Write dimension mismatch spec test: complex_voltage_current_mismatch_returns_undef вЂ” build voltage_dim and current_dim (DimensionVector::CURRENT), call eval_builtin("complex", &[Scalar{3, voltage}, Scalar{4, current}]), assert result is Undef because Voltage в‰  Current dimensions.

Write phase degree-equivalent verification test: phase_1_plus_i_approx_45_deg вЂ” create Complex{1,1,DIMLESS}, call phase(), assert result is Scalar with ANGLE dimension and si_value в‰€ ПЂ/4 в‰€ 0.7854 radians (which is 45В°). Add comment noting 45В° equivalence. This verifies the specific task-spec case phase(1+i)в‰€45deg.

Add LSP completion test: in crates/reify-lsp/src/completion.rs tests, verify that BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS contains complex number function names. Assert BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS.contains(&"complex"), .contains(&"real"), .contains(&"imag"), .contains(&"conjugate"), .contains(&"complex_magnitude"), .contains(&"phase"). This will fail until step-11 adds them.

Update BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS array in crates/reify-lsp/src/completion.rs to include complex number functions: add "complex", "real", "imag", "conjugate", "complex_magnitude", "phase" to the existing array. Keep existing entries and add a comment grouping the complex functions.

design_decisions.decision: Add `real`/`imag` as aliases alongside existing `re`/`im` using Rust `|` match patterns, keeping backward compatibility
design_decisions.rationale: Existing code and tests using `re`/`im` continue to work. The `|` pattern avoids code duplication. Both short and long names are common conventions in engineering tools (MATLAB uses `real`/`imag`, numpy uses `.real`/`.imag`). Short names `re`/`im` may be preferred in expression-heavy contexts.

design_decisions.decision: `complex_magnitude` is a Complex-only function separate from the existing `magnitude`
design_decisions.rationale: The generic `magnitude` handles both Tensor (vector norm) and Complex. A dedicated `complex_magnitude` provides a clearer typed API that rejects non-Complex inputs with Undef, matching the task spec's intent of a typed Complex function set. The generic `magnitude` continues to handle both types for backward compatibility.

design_decisions.decision: Voltage dimension is computed in tests as MASSВ·LENGTHВІВ·TIMEвЃ»ВіВ·CURRENTвЃ»В№ rather than adding a VOLTAGE constant
design_decisions.rationale: DimensionVector constants are defined for SI base dimensions only (LENGTH, MASS, TIME, CURRENT, etc.) in reify-types/dimension.rs. Derived dimensions like Voltage are composed from base dimensions using .mul()/.div()/.pow() arithmetic. Adding a VOLTAGE constant would belong in reify-types if needed globally, which is out of scope for this stdlib task.

design_decisions.decision: Steps 7-9 are test-only (no paired impl step) вЂ” they exercise existing implementations with task-spec scenarios
design_decisions.rationale: The underlying logic for complex constructor dimension matching, conjugate dimension preservation, magnitude on Complex, and phase computation already works correctly from task 288. The task spec requires specific Voltage and mismatch scenarios to be tested as verification. These tests should pass immediately with existing code, serving as regression guards.

design_decisions.decision: LSP BUILTIN_FUNCTIONS gets both alias names (`real`/`imag`) but not the short names (`re`/`im`)
design_decisions.rationale: The completion list is for user-facing autocomplete. The long names `real`/`imag` are more discoverable and self-documenting. The short names `re`/`im` are kept as implementation aliases but don't need to clutter the completion menu. Users who know the short names can type them directly.

reuse.how: Extend the match arms with `| "real"` and `| "imag"` to share the exact same closure вЂ” zero code duplication
Extract the Complex branch (if-let Complex pattern, sqrt(reВІ+imВІ), dimensionlessв†’Real / elseв†’Scalar) into the new `complex_magnitude` match arm, without the Tensor fallback that follows
Apply to `complex_magnitude` result, same pattern as existing magnitude/phase/complex_mul implementations
Wrap `complex_magnitude` implementation in `unary(args, |v| ...)` for consistent single-argument validation, same as all other unary complex functions
Use for all numeric assertions in new tests вЂ” assert_real_approx for dimensionless Real results, assert_scalar_approx for dimensioned Scalar results
Compose Voltage dimension in tests: `MASS.mul(&LENGTH.mul(&LENGTH)).div(&TIME.pow(3)).div(&CURRENT)`, using the same pattern as the existing complex_impedance_integration test

The Voltage dimension tests follow the same structure: build a derived dimension from base constants, create Complex with that dimension, test all accessors. Voltage is simpler than Impedance (V = kgВ·mВІВ·sвЃ»ВіВ·AвЃ»В№ vs О© = kgВ·mВІВ·sвЃ»ВіВ·AвЃ»ВІ)
